reviews.review_title: VR Banger that melts the eyes
reviews.review_description:
I've loved Thumper since I first played it... on my Go! Yes, the OG.

Plays and looks better than ever now with the Quest 3 ❤

reviews.review_title: Must play with Quest Game Optimizer
reviews.review_description: It looks perfect 4K 90hz with QCO. Please give it a try. It’s one of the best quest games ever.

reviews.review_title: Rhythm Violence in its definitive format
reviews.review_description:
This is actually the first game I have EVER played in VR, and after trying this game on the Nintendo Switch and mobile, I had to try it here. DROOL did a FANTASTIC job with this game. You're a beetle shooting down a track to either another day or your demise. Very simple mechanics, a high score/sement rating system that BEGS you to replay and actually master levels, and the fact that this game can be played for more than 3 hours? YES PLEASE!!
EDIT: This game is rated as "Comfortable," but be prepared for a VR hangover if go back to reality straight after playing, especially if you complete a full level at once... this is one of those that'll make you question if you have a body after...
Simple port let down by low resolution textures that stop it from looking beautiful glad I got this on sale, I know some people love this personally hardly ever gets played

The intense visuals, mesmerizing color 🌈scheme, the feeling of traveling at extreme ⚡velocity⚡, the music, the sound f/x, and game play... everything is mind-blowing🧠💥🤯 in this. 
The duo (Marc & Brian) kick a**👊😉No other game on my Quest 3 [or PC for that matter] comes close to providing this much entertainment. There's even a sense of a trance-like flow when I'm immersed in it.
And they do it all in less than 500MB, which is nothing short of miraculous... or genius on their part, actually. Games weighing in at 5GB [or 50GB for that matter] don't even come close to being as enjoyable [or dare I say as addicting] as THUMPER 🪲🐞 . 
If anyone seems to be getting stuck on any particular level or sub-level, just look online for a play-through vid, tutorial, etc ... and practice, practice, persevere. 
Take a chance on this one and get THUMP'd.🤜⚡🪲⚡🤛

I have been a fan of this game since the first PSVR and have since bought it on PSVR, PSVR2 and the Quest.
It is such a relaxing and beautiful game.
The gameplay is both simple and challenging.
The visuals you are bombarded with is a beautiful sight. 
The music just sucks you in and transports you somewhere else.

Turn out the lights, crank up the sound and be prepared to lose hours being absorbed into this experience.
I love everything about this game. The pace is wonderfully fast, the bosses are incredible, the visuals are spectacular in VR, the sound drawing you in deeper it's awesome, I'm hooked.

I have this on a few places - and this is the worst of them.

Can’t use a controller (according to store) and can’t remap left/right to something more responsive than stick - get this on a platform that lets you use a controller with analog or allows remapping.

(Playstation, PC or even Go!)

Pity this came in a bundle with something touch native, will have to refund both or be stuck with something I’ve uninstalled.

This game is just incredibly unique in VR. The atmosphere created through the visuals and music creates an amazing psychedelic experience. Aside from that the game itself is also fun and challenging. It really pulls you in deeply and keeps you engaged.
Just like the old Thumper game, but in VR, it is so much more immersive and hypnotic. 10/10.

if you’re reading you’re most likely wondering what the heck thumper is. well its kinda if guitar hero and heavy metal combined its cool its very challenging but very satisfying when you complete a level or beat a boss over all 8/10 because no endless mode so devs if you’re reading this add endless and will give 5 star
Psychedelic Masterpiece!  Love my time spent with Thumper!!!

Thumper, even in its flatscreen version, is a towering achievement in rhythm videogames: a relatively simple stick+button concept elevated to unbelievable heights through its satisfying gameplay loop and a delightfully unhinged presentation. Honestly, few games can boast such a wild premise: a futuristic-looking beetle racing into the depths of a hellish abyss, jumping and flying through countless enemies that act as “notes” in this bizarre symphony of violence. As you progress through the game’s 9 [circles of hell] levels, more gameplay mechanics get added into the mix, turning an already intricate rhythm game into a real reflex challenge.

In VR, however, you become one with the beetle. The sheer sense of speed and rhythm is unlike anything you could ever experience, let alone in a videogame. As you race through the trippy environments and hit perfect note combos, it’s like the brain unlocks some meditative state of pure bliss. And once it fully clicks, reality is all about getting you — the beetle — to the end of the line. Unreal stuff.

I had this on Switch before, but I never beat it. Somehow, it’s magically a lot easier with a screen strapped to my face!
It’s not really the traditional kind of rhythm game, but rather almost has more of a “call and response” kind of feel to it that really fits the music and atmosphere!
Anybody with a decent amount of experience with rhythm games could definitely make it through the whole thing, it’s really forgiving with the checkpoints and being able to regenerate your armor if you have a good combo going.
Overall, this was a great addition to my library, I’ll probably go back to get all S-ranks! :D

What can I say? It's weird, unique, fun, rewarding, and super underrated. Give it a spin because I promise you after a few minutes, you'll be hooked. It's super chill in VR. Love every aspect of this unique game. HIGHLY recommended!

DEVS!! I’VE BEEN PLAYING THIS FOREVER AND I CANNOT UNDERSTAND WHAT ACTIONS OR TIMING BREAKS THE WHEEL ON LEVEL 3!! THERE IS ZERO GUIDANCE ON THIS IN THE GAME AND I’M SICK OF IT. 5 STARS IF YOU CAN FIX IT. NOT FUN ANYMORE.

It took me a while to warm up to it, it seemed a bit simplistic for a VR game, but it’s now my go to cool-down game at the end of a VR session (since it’s seated). The music is awesome, oh and this would be great with a haptic vest if compatible (maybe it is?). It’s already very immersive, and really challenging.

I LOVE THIS GAME SOOOOO MUCH, I started playing before midnight ready for bed saw the daily deal and it looked cool, was like if it's garbage I can return it right quick and crash, but NO! This game had me glued in from the start, I ended up playing until 2 am and had to make myself turn it off. Long story short if you don't buy this, you're crazy!

When I first bought Thumper I tried it for about 30 minutes or so before requesting a refund. The game felt way too intense and I couldn’t see myself playing it on my Quest 2. I even felt slight uneasy of all the movements. It was just too crazy for me and it sure didn’t feel like a typical VR game. This coming from many years of using VR devices (since DK2 days).

But then something happened.

Fast forward about 24h and I found myself regretting my refund. I even watched some youtube clips to see some gameplay, and to figure out what it was that was so compelling. And what bothered me was that I couldn’t (or shouldn’t) re-buy a game during a refund request. 

About a week later I was back in and wow, this game is really a piece of art. My brain probably just needed to crunch the first 30 min overnight or something. Now I just love everything about it - the musical rythm, electronica vibes, visual art, the gameplay, the speed. No strange feelings or anything. This is a very unique game and it’s really an awesome experience. Feels like a timeless classic. And very addictive.

So if you do buy this game/experience, give it at least a week before settling on if this game is for you or not. :-) This together with Tetris Effect are the perfect combo for relaxing, seated, trippy games for VR.

Wanted improvements:
- I would love to see a resolution update for Quest 2, but it’s not a showstopper,
- Support for 3DoF / disabled tracking to allow for playing the game laying down or on an airplane.

... It is

Flow....

... It is

Play...

You shall...
This game sucks and I couldn’t get a refund.

When I just want to sit in my recliner, chill, and play an incredible game, this is what I pick. I love how Thumper teaches you how to play... rewards you for doing well...and punishes you for your mistakes. In my opinion, this is a must buy.
Don't waste your time or money. Boring and pointless. Maybe someone who's high will enjoy the psychedelic colors. You do not move your body at all. I just sit and watch.
There were none.  Didn’t make sense how to play.
If you're looking for a game to test your reflexes and suck you into a sci fi alien hellscape, this is the game for you. It's challenging, yet not frustrating, and the visuals are stunning.
This game is mind blowing , very exciting , doesent get much better than this , it's now my favourite game to play , I'll rate this game 11 out of 10 , very glad I purchased this .
This game is really something special. If you want an interesting (and at times frightening) experience, give this a go. The naysayers out there just don't understand what magic is.

reviews.review_title: You'll never play a game like this again
reviews.review_description:
And that's a shame! A unique experience and something you'll always go back to, easy to play but hard to master, thumper doesn't hold your hand. 

I've beaten this game on other platforms and the quest 2 does an amazing job bringing it into VR. 

If you love rhythm games, thumper is a must, the OST is absolutely fantastic, the controls are intuitive, and the graphics are beautiful.

Thumper is something a find myself delving back into on a regular basis, a truly unique game and perhaps something everyone should experience.
By far i must say that this game checks all the hashes for a good rhythm game, all the while feeling like a total LSD trip. Best rhythm game, maybe even the best game in vr that ive tried
Synaptic modulation, dopamine ~45^Run8.pdf Mental Capacity, Psycocrytic S++ [✓] True
An insane looking game, it gets very difficult towards later levels. My only complaint is you don't do much beyond sit in one place.

And amazing game that doesn’t require to stand up , you can simply sit on the couch and then enjoy the ride !!! 
Great music and amazing speed , fun and tensed ! Totally addicted and very well made !! Thanks !!
Love love love it.
Great game, but too fast for me!
It's intense, dark, robotic yet organic. The way the game works is it let's you hear a rhythm and you have to duplicate it. Very fast and cool towards the end.
Amazing visauls easy but hard game play headphones a must love it.

reviews.review_title: EVEN BETTER IN VR GET 25% OFF…
reviews.review_description:
I have this game on my mobile phone but it’s on another level playing in VR. Probably the best rhythm shooter game I’ve played. Fantastic visuals and addictive gameplay throughout.

(delete space before com) or message me for full link

I played this game a long time ago on Switch but never beat it. I knew it was designed mostly for VR so I bought it on Oculus. This has to be the absolute best version of Thumper! I immediately felt transported right behind the beetle, twisting and turning in this high speed rhythm game. I absolutely love it!

Now, because its an older game, there are some graphical issues. It looks grainy at times, especially when there aren't many 3D rendered images around you but once I started getting into the game, I hardly notice it. It would be awesome to get a sequel!
This game is insane! Way more difficult than it looks but very exhilarating.
Looks pretty - gameplay is quite good, nothing to shout about.. would get 5 stars at a lower price
